findings.evidence: grep hit: line 121 `change (((conjMulEquiv k hqr) ((conjMulEquiv h hpq) (φ : Deck p)) : Deck r).1 x) =`; nearby existing API has `Deck.conjMulEquivTrans` in `Deck/Conjugation.lean` for this composition.
findings.fix: Prove/reuse an explicit lemma from `conjMulEquivTrans` for the normalizer representative equality, or add a short comment documenting why the proof must descend to this pointwise `change` before `simp`.
findings.issue: `normalizerQuotientConjEquiv_trans_mk_congr` uses `change` to expose the pointwise shape of composed `conjMulEquiv`s after quotient/subtype congruence, with no comment explaining why this definally-shaped goal is unavoidable.

summary: One proof uses an undocumented `change` to force a non-obvious definitional equality through deck/normalizer quotient wrappers. That should be replaced by an explicit composition lemma or documented if no better route exists.
